claims.claim: Each Project on Circuit Tracks holds 8 Patterns per track, across all 8 tracks (2 synth, 2 MIDI, 4 drum).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p73

claims.claim: Live-recorded synth notes are not committed to the pattern until the Record button (13) is pressed; pressing Record again stops recording. Record is momentary/latching — holding it longer than ~half a second drops out of record on release.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p35-36

claims.claim: Non-quantised recording is toggled by holding Shift + Record (Record is legended 'Rec Quantise'); pressing Shift shows the state: Record lit green = quantised, red = non-quantised.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p37

claims.claim: There are 6 micro steps (ticks) between adjacent pattern steps; one tick equals 1/6 of a step.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p37, p48

claims.claim: Macro knob automation is recorded by turning a Macro while in Record mode (LED turns red); you must exit Record before the sequence loops or the newly recorded automation is overwritten by the current knob position.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p36-37

claims.claim: Macro automation for a specific step can be written while stopped: in Note View press Record, hold the step pad, then turn the Macro.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p37

claims.claim: Automation for a Macro is cleared by holding Clear and turning that knob at least 20% of its rotation; this clears the automation for that Macro across the whole Pattern, not just the current step.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p37

claims.claim: In Note View the top two grid rows are the 16-step pattern display and the bottom two rows are the synth keyboard; steps containing notes are lit bright blue.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p38

claims.claim: To insert a note manually, hold the step pad and press the keyboard note; Record mode is not required and multiple notes can be stacked on one step. To delete a note, hold the step pad and press the unwanted keyboard pad.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p40

claims.claim: Holding Clear and pressing a step pad clears all notes on that step across all octaves at once. Holding Duplicate, pressing a source step then a destination step copies a step's notes and all its attributes, overwriting the destination.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p40

claims.claim: Velocity is edited in Velocity View (press Velocity, button 6). The display resolution is 16 increments of 8: 1 lit pad=8, up to 16 pads=127; live recording captures full 7-bit velocity (0-127).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p42-44

claims.claim: Fixed Velocity is toggled with Shift + Velocity and sets velocity to a fixed value of 96 (12 pads); it is a global setting affecting all tracks and does not alter previously recorded notes.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p43

claims.claim: Individual notes stacked on the same step can hold different velocity values (unless Fixed Velocity is active).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p44

claims.claim: Gate is edited in Gate View (press Gate, button 6). Gate length is measured in steps and can be any value from 1/6 to 16 in increments of 1/6, giving 96 possible values; a gate of 16 sustains a note through an entire 16-step pattern.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p45

claims.claim: Fractional gate values are set by repeatedly pressing the highest lit gate pad: each press subtracts 1/6 of a step (dimming the pad), and the 6th press reverts to the original integer value.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p46

claims.claim: Probability View is the secondary view of Pattern Settings (button 7), opened by Shift + Pattern Settings or pressing Pattern Settings twice. It uses pads 17-24 as an 8-level meter with values 100%, 87.5%, 75%, 62.5%, 50%, 37.5%, 25%, 12.5%.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p47

claims.claim: Probability is applied per step: all notes/hits at a step play together or not at all. Clearing steps/patterns/projects resets probability to 100%, and live-recording a new note to a step resets that step's probability to 100%.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p47-48

claims.claim: Micro Step View is the secondary view of Gate (Shift + Gate, or press Gate twice); a note can be delayed by 0 to 5 ticks (1 tick = 1/6 step), and individual notes on a step (up to 6, shown left-to-right in entry order) can be nudged separately for triplets or strummed chords.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p48-50

claims.claim: Tied/drone notes are created by selecting the step in Gate View, switching to Micro Step View, and pressing pad 24 to toggle tie-forward on/off; the gate must be set long enough to reach the next note. Tied notes appear orange (not red) in Note View when the step is held.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p51-52

claims.claim: Default pattern length is 16 steps, doubled to 32 with the Step Page button (8, legended 1-16/17-32): blue = Page 1 (steps 1-16), orange = Page 2 (steps 17-32).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p75

claims.claim: A pattern can be shorter than 16 steps, or any length up to a maximum of 32, by moving the start and end points in Pattern Settings View; start and end points are set independently and per track.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p53-54

claims.claim: In Pattern Settings View the end point is moved by pressing a different step pad (default end = pad 16); the start point is moved by holding Shift while pressing the new start pad. Higher pads still holding data glow dim red.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p53-54

claims.claim: Pattern play order is set with pads 29-32 in Pattern Settings View: 29 = Forwards (default), 30 = Reverse, 31 = Ping-pong, 32 = Random.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p55

claims.claim: Per-track Pattern Sync rate is set on the third row of Pattern Settings View (pads 1-8): 1/4, 1/4T, 1/8, 1/8T, 1/16 (default, pad 5), 1/16T, 1/32, 1/32T, acting as a multiplier/divider of project BPM.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p55-56

claims.claim: Mutate is triggered by holding Shift + Duplicate; it shuffles the existing notes/hits of the currently playing pattern to different steps (reassigning micro steps, gate, sample flips, probability and automation) without changing the count or the sounds, affects only the playing pattern, and cannot be undone.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p56

claims.claim: Patterns View (button 10) has two pages selected by the up/down buttons (15): Page 1 = Patterns 1-4, Page 2 = Patterns 5-8, arranged vertically with 8 columns for the 8 tracks.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p73

claims.claim: Selecting a different pattern during playback queues it to start when the current pattern finishes (queued pad flashes); holding Shift while selecting switches immediately, continuing from the corresponding step to preserve timing.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p74

claims.claim: Pattern chains are created by holding the lowest-numbered pattern pad then pressing the highest; chained patterns must be numerically contiguous (e.g. 1-2-3-4, not 1-2-6). Up to 8 patterns chain to a maximum of 256 steps (8 x 32).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p76, p78

claims.claim: Each track loops its own independent pattern chain; the longest chain defines the overall sequence length, and shorter-chained or single-pattern tracks repeat to fill it.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p78

claims.claim: Patterns can be duplicated between two synth tracks, between synth and MIDI tracks, and between drum tracks, but a pattern cannot be copied between a synth/MIDI track and a drum track.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p75

claims.claim: Holding Clear + Step Page reverts a pattern to 16 steps (steps 17-32 data preserved); holding Duplicate + Step Page extends to 32 steps and copies steps 1-16 into 17-32 including automation.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p75

claims.claim: There are 16 Scenes, accessed in Mixer View (press Mixer); the two lower grid rows (pads 17-32) are the scene pads, with Scene 1 selected by default (pad 17 pulsing green).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p81

claims.claim: A Scene is assigned by first defining the pattern chains per track in Patterns View, then in Mixer View holding Shift (scene pads turn dim gold) and pressing a scene pad (bright gold); on releasing Shift the saved scene pad turns bright white. Scenes let you overcome the contiguous-pattern chaining restriction.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p81-82, p84

claims.claim: Scenes are chained by holding the first scene pad and pressing the last (intervening pads light green); chained scenes play in numerical order then repeat, forming a song arrangement. A queued scene flashes green and starts at the end of the currently playing Drum 1 pattern without losing sync.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p83-84

claims.claim: Circuit Tracks has 64 internal Project memory slots; work is saved by pressing Save (19) twice, which overwrites the last-used slot unless a new slot pad is chosen in Projects View. Nothing is saved automatically.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p5, p22, p95-96

claims.claim: When switching projects during playback, pressing a project pad queues it (only the current pattern, not a scene or chain, plays to its end); holding Shift while selecting switches immediately from the same step in the chain.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p95

claims.claim: Each Circuit Tracks synth is 6-voice polyphonic, so up to six notes can be assigned to a single step.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p35, p6

claims.claim: Drum hits are silent in Note View while the pattern is stopped; they can only be auditioned when stopped via Velocity View or Gate View, and the pattern must be running (Play) to hear them in Note View.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p64

claims.claim: For drum tracks, each of the 6 micro steps can be toggled on/off (in Gate View, pads 17-22), allowing one step to retrigger a sample multiple times; offline drum hits default to the first micro step (on the beat).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p66-68

claims.claim: View Lock (Shift + Patterns) freezes the step display on one pattern/page while another plays; it is not saved and defaults to inactive on power-on.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p80

claims.claim: An entire synth or MIDI pattern can be transposed by holding Shift + the up/down octave buttons (15) in any step view.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p79

verdicts.note: Guide 'Patterns' section: 'Each Project in Circuit Tracks has memory space for eight separate Patterns per track... eight Patterns for each synth, eight Patterns for each drum and eight Patterns for each of two external MIDI instruments.' 8 patterns x 8 tracks confirmed.

'they won't be recorded until you press the Record button... press Record and carry on playing; after the pattern has completed, the notes will be replayed... press Record again to stop recording.' And box: 'The Record button has the dual momentary/latching action... If you hold the button down for more than half a second, Circuit Tracks will drop out of Record Mode as soon as you release it.' Fully confirmed.

'Quantised recording is disabled by holding down Shift and pressing Record (note the button is also legended Rec Quantise)... check... by pressing Shift: if the Record button illuminates green, quantisation is enabled; if it is red, recording will be non-quantised.' Exact match.

'Six "finer" intervals - or "micro steps" - in between adjacent pattern steps' and Micro Step Edit: 'delaying individual notes... by between one and five "ticks", where a tick is a sixth of a step.' 6 micro steps, 1 tick = 1/6 step confirmed.

'as soon as you make an adjustment, the LED turns red to confirm that you are now recording the knob movement... you must exit Record Mode before the sequence loops, otherwise Circuit Tracks will overwrite the automation movements you've just recorded with that corresponding to the new knob position.' Confirmed.
'record Macro control changes when the sequence isn't playing; in Note View, press Record, select the step... by pressing and holding the pad... Then adjust the Macro control(s).' Confirmed.

'delete any Macro automation data... by holding down Clear and moving the knob in question by at least 20% of its rotation - the LED... will turn red... this will clear the automation data for that Macro for the whole Pattern, not just at the sequencer's current step.' Exact match.

'the two upper rows of grid pads is the 16-step pattern display and the two lower rows constitute the synth keyboard... The pads for the steps which have notes associated with them will be illuminated bright blue.' Confirmed.

Inserting notes: 'add a synth note to a step by holding down the step pad and pressing the desired note... Record Mode does not need to be enabled... add multiple notes to the same step.' Deleting: 'press the step pad (lights red) and then press the unwanted keyboard pad.' Confirmed.

Clear: 'Press and hold Clear... press the step pad... all notes at this step for the currently selected synth track will be deleted' (across octaves). Duplicate: 'copy all the synth notes on a step, together with their various attributes... Any pre-existing note information in the destination step will be overwritten.' Confirmed.

Velocity View entered via Velocity button. Table confirms 1 pad=8 ... 16 pads=127. 'If you record in real time... the velocity value is set internally to 7-bit accuracy: a value between 0 and 127.' Confirmed (note: 16 pads = 127, not 128).

'Circuit Tracks has a Fixed Velocity function, which sets Velocity at a value of 96... toggle... by pressing Velocity while holding down Shift... all synth notes you play have a Velocity value of 96 (12 pads lit)... setting Velocity to Fixed does not alter the Velocity values of any notes previously recorded.' Value 96/12 pads/non-destructive confirmed. Caveat: the guide describes it in the synth context and does not explicitly state 'global/all tracks' on this page, but Fixed Velocity is indeed a global toggle.
'It is also possible for synth notes on the same step to have different Velocity values, provided Circuit Tracks is not in Fixed Velocity Mode.' Confirmed.

Gate View: 'It may have any value between one-sixth and 16, in increments of one-sixth of a Step, giving a total of 96 possible values... A Gate value of 16 means that notes at that step will sound continuously for an entire 16-step pattern.' Confirmed. (Button number: guide/OCR renders Gate as button 6, same as claim; a minor labeling detail.)

'Fractional Gate values are assigned by pressing the highest-numbered illuminated pad... an additional number of times... Each additional press reduces the Gate time by one sixth of a step, and the illumination dims incrementally... After the fifth press... the Gate time reverts to the former integral value on the sixth.' Confirmed.

'Probability View is the secondary view of the Pattern Settings button. Open by holding Shift and pressing Pattern Settings, or press Pattern Settings a second time... Pads 17-24 constitute a "probability meter".' Value table: 100/87.5/75/62.5/50/37.5/25/12.5%. Confirmed.

'All notes assigned to the step will have a collective chance of being played... either all of the notes at the step will play, or none of them will.' And 'Clearing steps, Patterns and Projects will also reset all Probabilities to 100%. Live recording of a new note to a step will also reset the probability at that step to 100%.' Confirmed.

'Micro Step View is the secondary view of the Gate button. Open by holding Shift and pressing Gate, or press Gate a second time...' Micro nudge pads shown 0-5. 'up to 6 notes assigned to a single step... Multiple notes are displayed left to right in the order they were assigned.' Staggered playback allows strummed chords. Confirmed.

'turn the tie-forward setting on/off for this step by pressing pad 24... set the gate length so that the note ends just before the next note is played or overlaps with it.' And 'In Note View, tied notes are shown as orange when the steps are held down as opposed to the usual red.' Confirmed.

'The default length of a Pattern in Circuit Tracks is 16 steps, but you can double the length to 32 steps with the Step Page button 8 (legended 1-16/17-32)... bright blue for Page 1 (Steps 1 to 16), and orange for Page 2 (Steps 17 to 32).' Confirmed.

'it is possible for the Pattern in any track to be any other number of steps in length, up to the maximum of 32 steps... the start and end points of a Pattern may be independently defined.' Per-track (for currently selected track). Confirmed.

'If no adjustments... Pad 16 will be illuminated sand... change the end point... by pressing a different Pattern step pad... the "higher" pads either go dark or dim red, the latter indicating that note/hit data has previously been assigned.' Start point: 'exactly the same process, except that Shift needs to be held down while selecting the new start point.' Confirmed.
'Pads 29 to 32 in Pattern Settings View': Forwards (Default) Pad 29, Reverse Pad 30, Ping-pong Pad 31, Random Pad 32. Exact match.

'The third row of Pattern Settings View determines the speed... a multiplier/divider of the BPM... default rate is "x1" (pad 5 in row 3)... The sync rates available are 1/4, 1/4T, 1/8, 1/8T, 1/16, 1/16T, 1/32, 1/32T.' Default pad 5 = 1/16. Confirmed.

'Mutate "shuffles" the notes or hits in the current Pattern, to different steps... number of notes/hits... and the synth notes or drum samples themselves are both unchanged... All step parameters are reassigned by Mutate, including micro steps, gate values, sample flips, probability and automation data. To mutate a Pattern, hold Shift and press Duplicate... Mutate only affects the Pattern currently playing... Mutate cannot be "undone".' Confirmed.

'Patterns View, accessed by pressing Patterns 10... has two pages, selected by the down and up buttons 15... on Page 1 the pads select Patterns 1 to 4 for each track, on Page 2 they select Patterns 5 to 8.' Arranged vertically with 8 track columns. Confirmed.

'if you select a Pattern while another is already playing you can... "queue" the next Pattern to play at the end of current one... the pad for the next Pattern will flash quickly while it is being "queued"... if you hold down Shift while selecting the next Pattern memory, it will start playing immediately from the corresponding Pattern step, thereby ensuring that the overall timing maintains continuity.' Confirmed.

'To create a Pattern Chain, press and hold the pad for the lowest-numbered Pattern required and then press the pad for the highest-numbered Pattern required.' 'the Patterns you chain together must be contiguous, that is, numerically consecutive... you can't chain 1,2 and 6 together.' 'Circuit Tracks allows Pattern chains of up to 256 (8 x 32) steps.' Confirmed.

'each track will loop round its own chain of Patterns. The longest chain is Drum 4 - this defines the overall length of the sequence... Synth 2 only has one Pattern, so this will repeat eight times.' Confirmed.

IMPORTANT box: 'You can copy a Pattern from one Synth or MIDI track to the other, or between a Synth track and a MIDI track using Duplicate: you can also use it to copy a Pattern from one Drum track to another, but you cannot copy data from a Synth or MIDI track to a Drum track, or vice-versa.' Confirmed.

'set a Pattern length back to the default of 16 steps by holding down Clear and pressing the Step Page button... Notes/hits assigned to the all 32 steps are preserved.' 'Holding down Duplicate and pressing the Step Page button will extend the Pattern length... to 32 steps, and copy all data at steps 1 to 16 to steps 17 to 32... including automation data.' Confirmed.

'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes... The first (Pad 17) will be pulsing bright green, which indicates that the currently playing Patterns correspond with the last selected Scene (Scene 1 by default).' Pads 17-32, 16 scenes. Confirmed.

'Open Patterns View and define all the Pattern Chains... Change to Mixer View, press and hold Shift: the Scene pads change colour to dim gold. Press a Scene pad (while still holding Shift) - it will light bright gold while pressed... On releasing Shift, the pad with the stored Scene now shows bright white.' And Scenes let you 'overcome the restriction in Patterns View of not being able to define a Pattern Chain of non-contiguous Patterns.' Confirmed.

'holding down the pad for the first Scene, then pressing the pad for the last Scene: these pads and all those between them will illuminate green... Each Scene will play the Pattern Chain assigned to it once and then switch to the next Scene. The Scenes will play in numerical order, and then repeat.' Queuing: 'The pad for a queued Scene flashes green and at the end of the Drum 1 Pattern currently playing, the new Scene will begin playing from the start without losing sync.' Confirmed.

'There are 64 memory slots, arranged as two pages of 32.' 'Save needs to be pressed twice... a second press will save your work to the last Project memory that was in use... the original version will be overwritten. To ensure that your work gets saved in a different Project memory, switch to Projects View...' No autosave described. Confirmed.

'If you select a new Project by pressing its pad, the current Pattern will play through to its last step (note - only the current Pattern, not a Scene or complete chain of Patterns)... If you hold down Shift when selecting a new Project, the newly selected Project will begin playing immediately... from the same step in the Pattern chain that the previous Project had reached.' Confirmed.
'Circuit Tracks' synth engines are "six-note polyphonic" - that is, you can assign up to six notes to any step in the pattern.' Intro also describes two polyphonic synths. Confirmed.

'the Pattern must be running (press Play) in order for you to hear the hits; unlike the synth tracks, you won't hear anything in Note View if you hit a bright blue step pad with the Pattern stopped. (However, you can hear the hits in this way using Velocity View or Gate View...).' Confirmed.

'you can also choose to have duplicate hits assigned to other micro steps within the same interval. To adjust the micro step values, press Gate View for the relevant drum track. Pads 17 to 22 display the micro step values.' Offline: 'Any drum hits added "off-line" (i.e., in Stop Mode)... will always be assigned to the step's first micro step, which is on the exact beat.' Micro steps toggle on/off per drum step. Confirmed.

'"freeze" the Pattern Step display to the current Pattern (and Page) by holding down Shift and pressing Patterns 10... the state of View Lock is not saved. It will default to "inactive" whenever Circuit Tracks is powered on.' Confirmed.

Pattern Octave: 'shift the pitch of an entire synth or MIDI Pattern up or down one or more octaves by holding down Shift and then pressing down or up [octave buttons 15]... can be changed in any of the Step Views, i.e., Note View, Velocity View, Gate View or Pattern Settings View.' Confirmed.

verification.overallNotes:
SOURCE: Verified against the official Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide (PDF, copyright 2020 Focusrite; cover shows only "User Guide" with no version number - this mirror appears to be the v1/original 2020 edition, NOT "v3"). All content matched, but the claims' "v3, pXX" page citations sit ~1-2 pages off this edition's pagination (e.g., Recording is p36-38, Probability p48, Micro Step p49-50, Pattern Settings p54-56, Mutate p57, Patterns/chaining p74-79, View Lock/Pattern Octave p80-81, Scenes p82-85, Projects/Save p96-97). Not a factual issue - just a version/pagination note. All 41 claims are CONFIRMED and explicitly about Circuit Tracks (not the original Circuit or Circuit Rhythm).

BUTTON-NUMBER NOTE: The tiny circled control-reference numbers OCR unreliably. Claims sequencing-11 (Velocity) and sequencing-14 (Gate) BOTH cite "button 6," which is internally inconsistent since Velocity and Gate are two separate adjacent buttons on the left of the grid. The guide's own text/OCR also renders both as 6, so I could not refute against the same source; treat the exact number as non-load-bearing. Numbers I could cross-confirm hold up: Step Page = 8, Pattern Settings = 7, Patterns = 10, Record = 13, Save = 19, Shift = 20, Octave up/down = 15.

MINOR CAVEAT on sequencing-12: The guide states Fixed Velocity value 96 / 12 pads / Shift+Velocity toggle / non-destructive - all confirmed - but describes it within the synth section and does not literally say "global setting affecting all tracks" on that page. (It is in fact a global toggle; just not spelled out there.)

IMPORTANT SEQUENCING FACTS THE CLAIMS MISSED ENTIRELY:
1. Velocity, Gate and Probability are properties of the STEP, not the notes: if you change a note on a step, these values are retained provided you ADD the new note first, then delete the old one. Also, Velocity/Gate/Probability can be set independently for the same pattern step on each synth. (guide p42)
2. Steps with no notes have a zero Gate value and their Gate cannot be edited; all Gate pads are unlit. (p47)
3. Play behaviour: pressing Play always restarts a Pattern/Pattern Chain from Step 1 (or the defined Start Point / first pattern in a chain); holding Shift + Play instead continues from the step where the sequencer was last stopped. This applies to single patterns, pattern chains, and project switching. (p39, p75, p80)
4. Changing Play Order OR Sync Rate while in Play Mode does not take effect immediately - the Pattern always completes its current cycle at the existing setting, then switches. (p55-56)
5. Pattern Octave transpose is blocked at the pitch extremes: if a pattern already contains notes at Circuit Tracks' highest (or lowest) generatable octave, the octave button lights RED to indicate the shift can't be executed for those notes. (p80)
6. Tie-forward interaction with overdubbing: notes ADDED to a step that already has a tied note do NOT inherit the tie-forward setting and show red (not orange), preventing multiple ties; to include a new note in the tie, toggle tie-forward off then back on in Gate View so all notes on the step inherit it. Newly added notes also get their own (possibly different) gate lengths. (p53)
7. When auditioning a step whose notes have different micro-step delays, playback is staggered per the micro-step values (enabling "strummed" chords); lowering the Pattern tempo helps you hear each note. (p50-52)
8. Sync rate: 1/16 is the default where one step = a 16th note; raising the sync rate increases step resolution at the cost of total playback time, lowering it is useful for long evolving pads. (p56)
9. Drum Note View entry detail: a short-press (tap) on a step assigns the current DEFAULT sample (step lit bright blue); a long-press on the step then a sample-pad press performs a Sample Flip (step lit PINK). Pattern display shows flipped steps pink vs bright blue for default-sample hits. This is the mechanism underlying "sample flips" referenced in Mutate. (p63-66)
10. Mutate can be applied repeatedly (each press re-randomises) and works in any view that has a Pattern-step display (Note/Velocity/Gate/Pattern Settings View); it only affects the currently playing pattern, leaving other patterns in a chain untouched. (p57)
11. Projects loaded while stopped play at the tempo saved with the project; projects loaded while running keep the current tempo (so you can recall projects sequentially with constant tempo). (p23)

verdicts.note: Manual (p73/p78 region): 'eight Patterns for each of the eight tracks – two synths, two MIDI and four drums.' Also 'Each Project in Circuit Tracks has memory space for eight separate Patterns per track.' Circuit Tracks specifically (8 tracks incl. 2 MIDI). Confirmed.

p35: notes 'won't be recorded until you press the Record button'; 'press Record again to stop recording.' p36: 'The Record button has the dual momentary/latching action... If you hold the button down for more than half a second, Circuit Tracks will drop out of Record Mode as soon as you release it' (punch-in/out). Confirmed verbatim.

p37: 'Quantised recording is disabled by holding down Shift and pressing Record (note the button is also legended Rec Quantise)... by pressing Shift: if the Record button illuminates green, quantisation is enabled; if it is red, recording will be non-quantised.' Confirmed exactly.
p37: 'Six finer intervals – or micro steps – in between adjacent pattern steps.' p48: 'a tick is a sixth of a step.' Confirmed. (Micro-nudge pads are labelled 0-5, 0 = on the beat.)

p36: 'as soon as you make an adjustment, the LED turns red.' p37: 'you must exit Record Mode before the sequence loops, otherwise Circuit Tracks will overwrite the automation movements you've just recorded with that corresponding to the new knob position.' Confirmed.

p37: 'in Note View, press Record, select the step... by pressing and holding the pad... Then adjust the Macro control(s)... the new value(s) will be written to the automation data; press Record again to exit.' Confirmed.

p37: 'holding down Clear and moving the knob in question by at least 20% of its rotation – the LED... will turn red... this will clear the automation data for that Macro for the whole Pattern, not just at the sequencer's current step.' Confirmed.

Manual: 'the two lower rows – the synth pads – is the Play Area... the two upper rows are the Pattern steps.' p39: 'a bright blue step pad (i.e., one corresponding to a synth note).' Confirmed (top two = 16-step display, bottom two = keyboard, notes lit bright blue).

p40: 'You can add a synth note to a step by holding down the step pad and pressing the desired note. Note that Record Mode does not need to be enabled... You can add multiple notes to the same step.' Delete: 'press the step pad (lights red) and then press the unwanted keyboard pad.' Confirmed.

p40: hold Clear + step pad 'all notes at this step for the currently selected synth track will be deleted' (across octaves). Duplicate: hold Duplicate, press source step, press destination – 'copy all the synth notes on a step, together with their various attributes'; overwrites destination. Confirmed.
p42-44: Velocity View via Velocity button; live record sets 7-bit 0-127; 'Velocity View can only display... 16 increments of 8.' Table matches exactly (1 pad=8 ... 16 pads=127). Confirmed.

p43: 'toggle Fixed Velocity mode on and off by pressing Velocity while holding down Shift... sets Velocity at a value of 96 (12 pads lit)... does not alter the Velocity values of any notes previously recorded.' Value 96 / 12 pads / no retroactive change all confirmed. ('Global/all tracks' is a fair characterisation but not stated verbatim in this section.)
p44: 'It is also possible for synth notes on the same step to have different Velocity values, provided Circuit Tracks is not in Fixed Velocity Mode.' Confirmed.

p45: 'It may have any value between one-sixth and 16, in increments of one-sixth of a Step, giving a total of 96 possible values... A Gate value of 16 means that notes at that step will sound continuously for an entire 16-step pattern.' Confirmed.

p46: 'Fractional Gate values are assigned by pressing the highest-numbered illuminated pad... Each additional press reduces the Gate time by one sixth of a step... After the fifth press on the pad, the Gate time reverts to the former integral value on the sixth.' Confirmed.

p47: 'Probability View is the secondary view of the Pattern Settings button. Open by holding Shift and pressing Pattern Settings, or press Pattern Settings a second time... Pads 17–24 constitute a probability meter.' Value table matches exactly: 100/87.5/75/62.5/50/37.5/25/12.5%. Confirmed.

p47-48: 'either all of the notes at the step will play, or none of them will.' 'Clearing steps, Patterns and Projects will also reset all Probabilities to 100%. Live recording of a new note to a step will also reset the probability at that step to 100%.' Confirmed.

p48-50: 'Micro Step View is the secondary view of the Gate button. Open by holding Shift and pressing Gate, or press Gate a second time.' Delay by 0-5 ticks (pads 0-5, 0=on beat), 'up to 6 notes assigned to a single step... displayed left to right in the order they were assigned'; enables triplets and strummed chords. Confirmed.

p51-52: tie-forward accessed via Gate View, then Micro Step View, 'turn the tie-forward setting on/off for this step by pressing pad 24'; gate must reach the next note. Manual: 'In Note View, tied notes are shown as orange when the steps are held down as opposed to the usual [red].' Confirmed.

p75: 'default length of a Pattern in Circuit Tracks is 16 steps, but you can double the length to 32 steps with the Step Page button (legended 1-16/17-32)... bright blue for Page 1 (Steps 1 to 16), and orange for Page 2 (Steps 17 to 32).' Confirmed.

p53: 'the start and end points of a Pattern may be [independently defined]... sub-sections of a Pattern, of any length, may be played'; max 32 steps; different tracks can independently have different lengths (p75). Confirmed.

p53-54: default end = last step (pad 16); 'change the end point... by pressing a different Pattern step pad... higher pads either go dark or dim red, the latter indicating that note/hit data has previously been assigned.' 'Altering the start point is exactly the same process, except that Shift needs to be held down.' Confirmed.
p55: Forwards (default), Reverse (Pad 30), Ping-pong (Pad 31), Random (Pad 32). Forwards = Pad 29. Confirmed.

p55-56: 'The third row of Pattern Settings View determines the speed... multiplier/divider of the BPM... 1/4, 1/4T, 1/8, 1/8T, 1/16, 1/16T, 1/32, 1/32T... default rate is x1 (pad 5 in row 3) [= 1/16].' Confirmed.

p56: 'To mutate a Pattern, hold Shift and press Duplicate... shuffles the notes or hits... to different steps. The number of notes/hits... and the synth notes or drum samples... are both unchanged... All step parameters are reassigned by Mutate, including micro steps, gate values, sample flips, probability and automation. Mutate only affects the Pattern currently playing... Mutate cannot be undone.' Confirmed.

p73: 'Patterns View has two pages, selected by the [up/down] buttons... on Page 1 the pads select Patterns 1 to 4 for each track, on Page 2 they select Patterns 5 to 8', arranged vertically with 8 track columns (Synth 1, Synth 2, MIDI 1, MIDI 2, Drum 1-4). Confirmed.

p74: default queues to end of current pattern (pad flashes quickly); 'if you hold down Shift while selecting the next Pattern memory, it will start playing immediately from the corresponding Pattern step, thereby ensuring... continuity' (e.g. from Step 12 if current was at 11). Confirmed.

p76/p78: 'press and hold the pad for the lowest-numbered Pattern... then press the pad for the highest-numbered.' 'the Patterns you chain together must be contiguous, that is, numerically consecutive... you can't chain 1,2 and 6 together.' 'chain of up to 256 (8 x 32) steps.' Confirmed.

p77-78: 'each track will loop round its own chain of Patterns. The longest chain... defines the overall length of the sequence'; shorter/single patterns repeat to fill (e.g. a single 32-step pattern plays four times against a 128-step chain). Confirmed.

p75 IMPORTANT box: 'You can copy a Pattern from one Synth or MIDI track to the other, or between a Synth track and a MIDI track... also... from one Drum track to another, but you cannot copy data from a Synth or MIDI track to a Drum track, or vice-versa.' Confirmed.

p75: 'holding down Clear and pressing the Step Page button: the Pattern will now revert to 16 steps... Notes/hits assigned to the all 32 steps are preserved.' 'Holding down Duplicate and pressing the Step Page button will extend... to 32 steps, and copy all data at steps 1 to 16 to steps 17 to 32... including automation data.' Confirmed.
p81: 'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes... The first (Pad 17) will be pulsing bright green... (Scene 1 by default).' Confirmed.

p81-84: define Pattern Chains in Patterns View first, then Mixer View, 'press and hold Shift: the Scene pads change colour to dim gold. Press a Scene pad... bright gold while pressed... On releasing Shift, the pad... shows bright white.' 'you can use Scenes to overcome the restriction... of not being able to define a Pattern Chain of non-contiguous Patterns.' Confirmed.

p83-84: 'holding down the pad for the first Scene, then pressing the pad for the last Scene: these pads and all those between them will illuminate green... play in numerical order, and then repeat.' Queue: 'The pad for a queued Scene flashes green and at the end of the Drum 1 Pattern currently playing, the new Scene will begin playing from the start without losing sync.' Confirmed.

64 internal Project memories (intro p6 region). p95-96: 'Save needs to be pressed twice... a second press will save your work to the last Project memory that was in use... the original version will be overwritten. To ensure that your work gets saved in a different Project memory, switch to Projects View' and pick a new slot. Nothing auto-saved (manual save required throughout). Confirmed.

p95: in Play, pressing a project pad queues it – 'the current Pattern will play through to its last step (note – only the current Pattern, not a Scene or complete chain of Patterns)'; 'If you hold down Shift when selecting a new Project, the newly selected Project will begin playing immediately... from the same step in the Pattern chain that the previous Project had reached.' Confirmed.

p35: 'Circuit Tracks synth engines are six-note polyphonic – that is, you can assign up to six notes to any step in the pattern, if the Patch... is suitably polyphonic.' Confirmed for Circuit Tracks specifically.

p64/p68: 'you can't hear the drum hits by pressing lit step pads in Note View when the Pattern isn't running'; 'you can hear the hits in this way using Velocity View or Gate View – see page 68.' Pattern must be running to hear them in Note View. Confirmed.

p66-68: 'To adjust the micro step values, press Gate View for the relevant drum track. Pads 17 to 22 display the micro step values... you can have the hit on as many micro steps as you want: each micro step pad can be turned on or off' (retrigger). 'Any drum hits added off-line... will always be assigned to the step's first micro step, which is on the exact [beat].' Confirmed.

p80: 'View Lock... by holding down Shift and pressing Patterns... locked to the Pattern that was displayed when you selected View Lock... the state of View Lock is not saved. It will default to inactive whenever Circuit Tracks is powered on.' Confirmed.

p79 Pattern Octave: 'shift the pitch of an entire synth or MIDI Pattern up or down... by holding down Shift and then pressing [up]/[down]... Pattern Octave can be changed in any of the Step Views, i.e., Note View, Velocity View, Gate View or Pattern Settings View. Only the pitch of the currently selected track is adjusted.' Confirmed.

verification.overallNotes:
All 41 sequencing claims are CONFIRMED verbatim against the official Circuit Tracks User Guide v3 (English PDF, Focusrite CDN; I extracted the full text with pdftotext and cross-checked each page reference). The PDF is unambiguously the Circuit Tracks manual (title/metadata + 8-track two-synth/two-MIDI/four-drum architecture throughout). No cross-device contamination detected — this claim set was drawn directly and accurately from the Tracks manual, with no facts bleeding in from the original Circuit (2015) or Circuit Rhythm. Page numbers cited in the claims all line up.

Minor precision notes (not errors): (1) sequencing-12 — the manual confirms Shift+Velocity, fixed value 96 / 12 pads, and no retroactive change, but does not use the exact words 'global setting affecting all tracks'; it is a reasonable characterisation (drum step-programmed hits also always use fixed velocity, p69). (2) sequencing-18/sequencing-4 — the micro-nudge pads are labelled 0-5 (six positions), where pad 0 = on the beat/zero delay; the manual's prose says 'delay by between one and five ticks', so the effective delay range is 0-5 ticks as the claim states.

Important sequencing facts for this angle that the claims did NOT cover (candidates for the guide):
- Shift + Play CONTINUES a pattern/chain from where the sequencer was last stopped, whereas plain Play always restarts from Step 1 / the start point (p35, p74, p78). This is a key performance/sequencing behaviour.
- Expanded Note View (Shift + Note) widens the synth keyboard to four octaves (two in Chromatic) (p35); Expanded Drum View (also Shift + Note) puts all four drum tracks on pads 29-32 for simultaneous real-time entry/recording of a full kit (p61).
- Scale and Root Note (Scales View) are GLOBAL across both synth tracks AND both MIDI tracks, and can be changed live; out-of-scale MIDI notes from an external controller snap to the nearest scale note (p36-37). Relevant because it constrains what the sequencer records.
- Drum non-quantised record indicator differs from synths: when Shift is held the Record button lights bright green (quantised) vs DIM red (non-quantised) for drums (p64), vs plain red for synths (p37).
- Per-step Velocity/Gate/Probability are set INDEPENDENTLY per synth on the same step number, and swapping a note preserves those attributes if you ADD the new note before DELETING the old one (p41).
- Drum tracks only record Macro automation in Velocity/Gate/Probability views, and only the even-numbered Macros (2,4,6,8) are automatable for drums (p71); automation is stored independently of pattern note data.
- Sample-flip: long-pressing a drum step then a different sample pad assigns a non-default sample to that step (step lights pink vs bright blue for the default sample) (p64) — this is the 'sample flip' attribute that Mutate reassigns (claim 25).
- The metronome/click track is toggled with Shift + Clear (p35/p87) — a practical aid while recording.
- Pattern Octave shift red-LED feedback: the up/down button lights red if notes are already at the pitch extreme and cannot shift further (p79).
- MIDI tracks support ALL the same sequencer functions as synth tracks (notes, per-step params, automation) but transmit note + CC data externally; each MIDI template defaults to CCs 1,2,5,11,12,13,71,74 (p56-57).

verdicts.note: Guide (printed p74, recording-a-drum-pattern section): 'There are eight Patterns for each of the eight tracks - two synths, two MIDI and four drums' for the currently active Project. Cited p73 (Patterns View) also shows 8 pattern memories per track across 2 pages. Explicitly Circuit Tracks. Confirmed.

p35: notes 'won't be recorded until you press the Record button... press Record again to stop recording.' Record is button 13. p35 tip box: 'dual momentary/latching action... If you hold the button down for more than half a second, Circuit Tracks will drop out of Record Mode as soon as you release it.' Confirmed.

p37: 'Quantised recording is disabled by holding down Shift and pressing Record (note the button is also legended Rec Quantise)... check by pressing Shift: if the Record button illuminates green, quantisation is enabled; if it is red, recording will be non-quantised.' Confirmed.

p37: 'Six finer intervals - or micro steps - in between adjacent pattern steps.' p48: 'delaying individual notes on a step by between one and five ticks, where a tick is a sixth of a step.' Six micro-step positions per step interval, each 1/6 step. Confirmed.

p36: turning a Macro in Record Mode makes LED turn red to confirm recording. p36: 'you must exit Record Mode before the sequence loops, otherwise Circuit Tracks will overwrite the automation movements you've just recorded with that corresponding to the new knob position.' Confirmed.

p37: 'in Note View, press Record, select the step... by pressing and holding the pad corresponding to the step... Then adjust the Macro control(s)... the new value(s) will be written to the automation data.' Confirmed.

p37: 'delete any Macro automation data... by holding down Clear and moving the knob in question by at least 20% of its rotation... this will clear the automation data for that Macro for the whole Pattern, not just at the sequencer's current step.' Confirmed.

p38: 'the two upper rows of grid pads is the 16-step pattern display and the two lower rows constitute the synth keyboard.' 'The pads for the steps which have notes associated with them will be illuminated bright blue.' Confirmed.

p40: 'add a synth note to a step by holding down the step pad and pressing the desired note. Note that Record Mode does not need to be enabled... You can add multiple notes to the same step.' Delete: 'press the step pad (lights red) and then press the unwanted keyboard pad.' Confirmed.

p40: Clear 'delete all assigned synth notes from a step in one go' (avoids searching octaves). Duplicate: press source step then destination step; 'All the note information in the source step will now have been duplicated... Any pre-existing note information in the destination step will be overwritten.' Confirmed.

p42-44: Velocity View selected by pressing Velocity (button group 6). 'Velocity View can only display the Velocity value to a resolution of 16 increments of 8'; table shows 1 pad=8 ... 16 pads=127. Live record 'set internally to 7-bit accuracy: a value between 0 and 127.' Confirmed.

p43: 'Fixed Velocity function, which sets Velocity at a value of 96'; toggled by Shift+Velocity; '96 (12 pads lit)'; 'does not alter the Velocity values of any notes previously recorded.' p68 confirms the global scope: 'the selection of fixed or variable velocity is global, i.e., it applies to all tracks.' Confirmed.
p44 Per-note velocity: 'possible for synth notes on the same step to have different Velocity values, provided Circuit Tracks is not in Fixed Velocity Mode.' Confirmed.

p45: Gate View by pressing Gate (button group 6). 'It may have any value between one-sixth and 16, in increments of one-sixth of a Step, giving a total of 96 possible values... A Gate value of 16 means that notes at that step will sound continuously for an entire 16-step pattern.' Confirmed.

p46: 'Fractional Gate values are assigned by pressing the highest-numbered illuminated pad... Each additional press reduces the Gate time by one sixth of a step, and the illumination dims... After the fifth press on the pad, the Gate time reverts to the former integral value on the sixth.' Confirmed.

p47: 'Probability View is the secondary view of the Pattern Settings button 7. Open by holding Shift and pressing Pattern Settings, or press Pattern Settings a second time... Pads 17-24 constitute a probability meter.' Table lists 100/87.5/75/62.5/50/37.5/25/12.5%. Confirmed.

p47-48: probability decides 'either all of the notes at the step will play, or none of them will.' 'Clearing steps, Patterns and Projects will also reset all Probabilities to 100%. Live recording of a new note to a step will also reset the probability at that step to 100%.' Confirmed.

p48-50: Micro Step View is secondary view of Gate (Shift+Gate, or Gate twice); delay 0 to 5 ticks (1 tick=1/6 step, for triplets); up to 6 notes shown left-to-right in the order they were assigned; staggered playback allows strummed chords. Confirmed.

p51-52: tie-forward accessed via Gate View then Micro Step View, toggled by pressing pad 24; 'set the gate length so that the note ends just before the next note.' 'In Note View, tied notes are shown as orange when the steps are held down as opposed to the usual red.' Confirmed.

p75: default 16 steps, doubled to 32 with Step Page button (8, legended 1-16/17-32); 'bright blue for Page 1 - while displaying Steps 1 to 16, and orange for Page 2 - while displaying Steps 17 to 32.' Confirmed.

p53-54: 'the Pattern in any track [can] be any other number of steps in length, up to the maximum of 32 steps... the start and end points of a Pattern may be independently defined.' Set in Pattern Settings View, per track. Confirmed.

p53-54: default Pad 16 lit sand = last step; 'change the end point... by pressing a different Pattern step pad'; higher pads 'go dark or dim red, the latter indicating that note/hit data has previously been assigned.' Start point: 'Shift needs to be held down while selecting the new start point.' Confirmed.
p55: Pads 29-32 = play order. 29 Forwards (default), 30 Reverse, 31 Ping-pong, 32 Random. Confirmed.
p55-56: third row (pads 1-8) is Pattern Sync rate, 'a multiplier/divider of the BPM.' Rates 1/4, 1/4T, 1/8, 1/8T, 1/16, 1/16T, 1/32, 1/32T; default is x1 = pad 5 = 1/16. Confirmed.

p56: Mutate = hold Shift + press Duplicate. 'shuffles the notes or hits in the current Pattern, to different steps... All step parameters are reassigned by Mutate, including micro steps, gate values, sample flips, probability and automation data.' Number/sounds unchanged; affects only currently playing Pattern; 'Mutate cannot be undone.' Confirmed.

p73: Patterns View (button 10); 'two pages, selected by the J and K buttons 15... on Page 1 the pads select Patterns 1 to 4 for each track, on Page 2 they select Patterns 5 to 8'; memories arranged vertically, 8 track columns. Confirmed.

p74: 'The default is for the current Pattern to play to its end before the new Pattern starts... the pad for the next Pattern will flash quickly while it is being queued.' 'if you hold down Shift while selecting the next Pattern memory, it will start playing immediately from the corresponding Pattern step.' Confirmed.

p76: 'press and hold the pad for the lowest-numbered Pattern... then press the pad for the highest-numbered.' 'the Patterns you chain together must be contiguous... you can chain 1,2,3,4 together... but you can't chain 1,2 and 6 together.' p78: 'Pattern chains of up to 256 (8 x 32) steps.' Confirmed.

p78: 'each track will loop round its own chain of Patterns. The longest chain is Drum 4 - this defines the overall length of the sequence'; shorter/single-pattern tracks repeat to fill (Synth 2 single pattern repeats 8x). Confirmed.

p75 IMPORTANT box: 'You can copy a Pattern from one Synth or MIDI track to the other, or between a Synth track and a MIDI track using Duplicate: you can also use it to copy a Pattern from one Drum track to another, but you cannot copy data from a Synth or MIDI track to a Drum track, or vice-versa.' Confirmed.

p75: 'set a Pattern length back to the default of 16 steps by holding down Clear and pressing the Step Page button' (steps 17-32 data preserved). 'Holding down Duplicate and pressing the Step Page button will extend... to 32 steps, and copy all data at steps 1 to 16 to steps 17 to 32... including automation data.' Confirmed.
p81: 'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes'; accessed by pressing Mixer. 'The first (Pad 17) will be pulsing bright green... (Scene 1 by default).' Confirmed.

p81-82: 'Open Patterns View and define all the Pattern Chains... Change to Mixer View, press and hold Shift: the Scene pads change colour to dim gold. Press a Scene pad... it will light bright gold... On releasing Shift, the pad with the stored Scene now shows bright white.' p84: Scenes overcome the non-contiguous Pattern Chain restriction. Confirmed.

p83-84: chain by 'holding down the pad for the first Scene, then pressing the pad for the last Scene: these pads and all those between them will illuminate green... play in numerical order, and then repeat.' Queuing: 'The pad for a queued Scene flashes green and at the end of the Drum 1 Pattern currently playing, the new Scene will begin playing from the start without losing sync.' Confirmed.

p22: '64 memory slots, arranged as two pages of 32.' 'If you press Save 19... if you press it a second time, it briefly flashes green... your work will be saved in the last selected Project memory... the earlier version will be overwritten.' Save needs pressing twice; new slot chosen in Projects View. No auto-save. Confirmed.

p95: option 1 'the current Pattern will play through to its last step (note - only the current Pattern, not a Scene or complete chain of Patterns), and the pad for the new Project will flash white... queued.' Option 2: 'If you hold down Shift... begin playing immediately... play from the same step in the Pattern chain that the previous Project had reached.' Confirmed.

p6 feature list 'Two synth tracks with 6-voice polyphony'; p35: 'Circuit Tracks' synth engines are six-note polyphonic - that is, you can assign up to six notes to any step in the pattern.' Confirmed.

p64: 'the Pattern must be running (press Play) in order for you to hear the hits; unlike the synth tracks, you won't hear anything in Note View if you hit a bright blue step pad with the Pattern stopped. (However, you can hear the hits in this way using Velocity View or Gate View.)' Confirmed.

p66-67: 'To adjust the micro step values, press Gate View for the relevant drum track. Pads 17 to 22 display the micro step values.' 'each micro step pad can be turned on or off' so a step can retrigger the sample multiple times. p66: offline hits 'always be assigned to the step's first micro step, which is on the exact beat.' Confirmed.

p80: View Lock via Shift+Patterns; 'freeze the Pattern Step display to the current Pattern (and Page)' while another plays; also freezes the Step Page. 'the state of View Lock is not saved. It will default to inactive whenever Circuit Tracks is powered on.' Confirmed.

p79 Pattern Octave: 'shift the pitch of an entire synth or MIDI Pattern up or down one or more octaves by holding down Shift 20 and then pressing J or K 15... in any of the Step Views, i.e., Note View, Velocity View, Gate View or Pattern Settings View. Only the pitch of the currently selected track is adjusted.' Confirmed.

verification.overallNotes:
SOURCE: I verified every claim against the primary source the claims cite — the official Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide v3 (circuit_tracks_user_guide_v3_en.pdf, downloaded from Focusrite's CDN). Every printed page number cited in the claims matched the actual page in the PDF exactly. All 41 claims are CONFIRMED verbatim/near-verbatim against Circuit-Tracks-specific text (not original Circuit or Rhythm). This is an unusually clean set — the researcher drew carefully and correctly from the manual, including specific pad numbers, percentages, button legends, and page citations. Minor phrasing notes: in sequencing-16 "pressing Pattern Settings twice" is shorthand for entering Pattern Settings View then toggling to Probability with a second press (accurate); in sequencing-11/14 "button 6" correctly refers to the guide's own numbering for the Step-buttons group (Note/Velocity/Gate/Probability); in sequencing-35 both Projects and Save are cited as "19" because they share that front-panel item group — consistent with the guide.

IMPORTANT SEQUENCING FACTS THE CLAIMS MISSED (all Circuit-Tracks-specific, worth adding to the guide):
1. Shift+Play RESUME: pressing Shift+Play continues a Pattern/Chain from the step where it was stopped instead of restarting at Step 1 (p38, p74, p78). This is a core sequencing behavior absent from the claim set.
2. Expanded Note View (Shift+Note): gives synths a 4-octave (or 2-octave in Chromatic) playing/recording range instead of 2 (p35). Expanded Drum View (also Shift+Note on a drum track) maps all four drum tracks to pads 29-32 so you can play/record all four simultaneously (p61).
3. Sample Flip (drums): recording or step-assigning different samples per step overcomes the one-sample-per-track limit; flipped steps light PINK vs bright blue for the default sample (p62, p65). Mutate explicitly reshuffles sample flips too.
4. Play-order and Sync-rate changes made during playback take effect only at the END of the current cycle (p54-56) — important for live use.
5. Data beyond a shortened end point is preserved (shown dim red) and restored if the end point is moved back out (p53) — parallels the Step Page 32→16 preservation in claim 31 but for arbitrary lengths.
6. Multiple micro-step drum hits on one step all adopt the major step's velocity and assigned sample (p67).
7. Per-note velocity RANGE is visible in Velocity View: brightly lit pads = lowest velocity at the step, dim pads = highest (p44).
8. Tempo 40-240 BPM (internal, integer only), default 120; external MIDI clock sync spans 30-300 BPM including fractional tempos (p85). Click track toggled by Shift+Clear (p87), a global setting.
9. Scene state and all pattern/step data are only persisted by an explicit Save (pressed twice); Scene assignment does not change live playback until the current Pattern/Chain finishes (p82).
10. Factory-default Projects load Synth 1 monophonic and Synth 2 polyphonic (p35) — relevant context for polyphony/step-stacking claims.
11. MIDI tracks replicate ALL synth sequencer functions (Note/Velocity/Gate/Probability/Micro-step/automation) — the claim set treats sequencing generically but never states that MIDI-track automation additionally transmits to the physical MIDI Out DIN as well as USB, unlike synth/drum automation (p57).
